key_insight: The paper extends Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector-based centrality from real positive networks to complex-weighted networks, enabling principled node-importance measures when edge weights are complex.
engineering_relevance: This provides a foundation for analyzing complex networked systems in quantum, electrical, chemical, and communication settings where standard real-valued centralities do not apply.
method: Generalize PF-style results to complex-valued matrices and compare related formulations.
Define and compute complex-weighted eigenvector-based centrality measures.
Construct examples from application domains to test existence and behavior of the generalized theory.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical proofs plus worked examples from electron transport, circuit analysis, mathematical chemistry, and communication networks, rather than a dataset-driven empirical evaluation.
watch_item: Complex eigenvectors and non-real spectra may complicate interpretation, normalization, and numerical stability when turning the theory into a usable centrality score.
paper_title: Generalizing Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector-based centralities to networks with complex edge weights
